en.extract:
The pale-winged starling (Onychognathus nabouroup) is a species of starling in the family Sturnidae. It is found in Angola, Botswana, Namibia, and South Africa.


Habitat

This starling is found in arid and semi-arid areas in South-western Africa, mainly in areas with rocky terrain, cliffs and gorges.


Diet
The pale-winged starling is a generalist omnivore, feeding on a range of fruit and insects.


Social Behavior
It is monogamous.


References


External links

Pale-winged starling - Species text in The Atlas of Southern African Birds
Pale-winged starling Structured guide to the species in southern Africa

The '''pale-winged starling''' ('''''Onychognathus nabouroup''''') is a species of [[starling]] in the family [[Sturnidae]]. It is found in [[Angola]], [[Botswana]], [[Namibia]], and [[South Africa]].

==Habitat==
[[File:Pale-winged starling (Onychognathus nabouroup).jpg|alt=Pale-winged starling in Damaraland, Namibia|left|thumb|Pale-winged starling in [[Damaraland]], Namibia]]
[[File:Pale-winged starling 2017 03 27 2601.jpg|thumb|left|Pale-winged starling in flight]]
This starling is found in arid and semi-arid areas in South-western [[Africa]], mainly in areas with rocky terrain, cliffs and gorges.

==Diet==
The pale-winged starling is a [[Generalist and specialist species|generalist]] [[omnivore]], feeding on a range of [[fruit]] and [[insect]]s.

== Social Behavior ==
It is monogamous.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Hausberger |first=Martine |last2=Giacalone |first2=Aline |last3=Harmand |first3=Mariane |last4=Craig |first4=Adrian J.F.K. |last5=Henry |first5=Laurence |date=2019-12-03 |title=Calling rhythm as a predictor of the outcome of vocal interactions: flight departure in pale-winged starling pairs |journal=The Science of Nature |volume=107 |issue=1 |doi=10.1007/s00114-019-1658-1 |issn=0028-1042}}</ref>
